content.full_text:
Fern Polrrrcal PRACrrcEs CourrrssroN
428 J Street o Suite 620 r Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
February 16,2005
Helen Holmes Peak, City Attorney
City of San Marcos
1 Civic Center Drive
San Marcos, CA 92069-2918
Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our FiIe No. W-05-008
Dear Ms. Peak
This letter is in response to your request for informal assistancel regarding the
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").2 This letter should not be construed
as assistance on any conduct that may have already taken place. In addition, this letter is
based on the facts presented. The Fair Political Practices Commission ("Commission,,)
does not act as a finder of fact when it renders assistance. (In re Oglesby (1g7 5) I FppC
Ops. 71; Govt. Code section 831 14.)
QUESTION
May Councilmember Hal Martin vote on the appointment of an applicant to the
city planning commission if the applicant is a campaign contributor to thai council
member, and if that applicant would otherwise be entitled to collect a meeting stipend as
a planning commission member, but expressly waives and disclaims any right to collect
such stipend?3
I Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or
formal written advice. (Section 83 1 14; regulation I 8329(c)(3), copy enclosed.j 
-
2 Government Code sections 8 1 000 - gl}14. Clrn*ir.lon regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the Califomia Code of Regulations. All statutory references are to the Government Code
unless otherwise indicated. All regulatory references are to Title 2 of the Code of Regulation unless
otherwise indicated-
t In yor. letter, the question you pose is based on a "source of income" economic interest in the
applicant' However, in a subsequent telephone conversation you indicated that Councilmember Martin
does not have a "source of income" economic interest in the applicant, as defined under the political
Reform Act, but that campaign contributions of $100 or *o.. u." dellned as "income,,under the local
ordinance.
Re:


File No. W-05-008
Page No. 2
CONCLUSION
Under the facts presented, you have not identified any factors which would
indicate that the applicant is an economic interest of Councilmember Hal Martin,s, as that
term is defined in the Act. Therefore, your request does not present a question under the
Act, or is purely hlpothetical. Accordingly, the Commission declines to provide written
advice or informal assistance regarding your question.
Section 83114, subdivision (a) allows any person to seek advice from the
Commission'kith respect to the person's duties under this title." Regulation l132g
states the Commission will assist persons in complying with the Act and will provide
formal written advice or informal assistance to facilitate compliance with the Act.
Regulation 18329, subdivisions (b)(8)(D) and (c)(a)(D and F) states that the Commission
may decline to give formal written advice or provide informal assistance, respectively,
when the request does not present a question under the Act or the question piesented is
purely hypothetical.
The Act specifically provides that campaign contributions are not considered
income or gifts. (Sections 82030, subdivision (b)(1), section S2)2S,subdivision (bX4).)
Under the facts you have presented, Councilmember Martin has no source of income
economic interest under the Act in the individual applicant for the planning commission
position. Accordingly, since you have not indicated an economic interest as defined
under the Act, your question does not present a question under the Act or the question
presented is purely hSpothetical. Therefore, we decline to answer the specific question
posed in your letter.
For your information, we have enclosed the pamphlet ,.can I vote,,, a guide to
preventing conflicts of interest forpublic officials. If you have any other questions
regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660-
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
By:
. Lenkeit
Counsel, Legal Division
Enclosures
wJLjg
I:\Adviceltrs W-\05 -008
